p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church both present and to come, Roger son of Glay sends greeting.

Know me to have given and granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God; for the salvation of Walter fitz Alan my lord, and for my own salvation, for the soul of my father and mother; certain land in the territory of Innerwick, as the ditch built by their brethren encloses the same land; in free, pure, and everlasting alms, released and quit from every earthly service and exaction, and secular demand, just as they freely, quitely, and honourably hold and possess any alms in the kingdom of Scotland. And I and my heirs shall warrant to the foresaid monks the same land against all men for ever. In addition I have given and granted, and by this my charter made firm, to the foresaid monks the common pasture of the same toun of Innerwick, from the low road to the head of the 'Manyneth', to those boundaries that are between us and the monks of Kelso, that is, transversely from the 'Monesclhat' to the 'Wytsclad', and from the 'Wytsclad' to the 'Edwardescloth', and from the 'Edwardescloth' to the 'Botkil', and so upwards by the same water, that is the 'Botkil', by the correct boundaries of Innerwick to the sheepfold of the foresaid monks, and from that sheepfold to the forenamed low road. The foresaid monks shall hold this whole land with easements within the foresaid boundaries, that is in peateries, in peat bogs, in heath ground, in pastures, of me and my heirs in pure and perpetual alms, freely, quitely, and released from all services, exactions and demands, just as any alms is freely and quitely and honourably held in the kingdom of Scotland.
With these witnesses: my lord, Walter fitz Alan; Roger Avenel; Hugh Mauleverer; Hugh Ridel; William de Heriz, and many others.
